DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS RIMMA BOROKHOV, DAVID TSE AND MICHAEL TO'S AMENDED MOTION TO ENFORCE SUBPOENA - THE COURT GRANTS THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO PARAGRAPHS 6, 8-12 AND 14-17. THE COURT OVERRULES THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS AS TO THE OTHER PARAGRAPHS. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE INFORMATION INVOLVES PRIVATE EMPLOYMENT RECORDS OF A THIRD PARTY. THEREFORE, THE RECORDS MUST BE DIRECTLY RELEVANT TO THE MATTER AND THE LEAST INTRUSIVE MEANS OF DISCOVERY. THE SUBPOENA IS NOT THE LEAST INTRUSIVE MEANS TO GET THE INFORMATION SOUGHT. THE COURT URGES THE PLAINTIFF TO TAKE THE DEPOSITION OF MR. TROTTER TO GARNER THE NECESSARY INFORMATION, HOWEVER, IF AFTER THE DEPOSITION, THE PLAINTIFF STILL FEELS THAT THESE RECORDS ARE NECESSARY, THE PLAINTIFF HAS THE RIGHT TO SUBPOENA THE RECORDS AND SEEK AN ORDER FROM THE COURT FOR PRODUCTION, IF NECESSARY. FURTHERMORE, THE COURT URGES THE PARTIES TO ENTER INTO AN APPROPRIATE PROTECTIVE ORDER SHOULD THE DOCUMENTS NEED TO BE PRODUCED TO PROTECT THE PRIVACY OF THIRD PARTY INFORMATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. PRO TEM JUDGE: ELIZABETH RILES, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/MJM

Amended Notice Of Motion To Enforce Subpoena
Off Calendar Mar-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
PLAINTIFFS RIMMA BOROKHOV, DAVID TSE AND MICHAEL TO'S Amended Motion To Enforce Subpoena -
The Court grants the request for judicial notice. The Court sustains the evidentiary objections to paragraphs 6, 8-12 and 14-17. The court overrules the evidentiary objections as to the other paragraphs. The court denies the motion without prejudice. The information involves private employment records of a third party. Therefore, the records must be directly relevant to the matter and the least intrusive means of discovery. The subpoena is not the least intrusive means to get the information sought. The Court urges the Plaintiff to take the deposition of Mr. Trotter to garner the necessary information, however, if after the deposition, the Plaintiff still feels that these records are necessary, the Plaintiff has the right to subpoena the records and seek an order from the Court for production, if necessary. Furthermore, the Court urges the parties to enter into an appropriate protective order should the documents need to be produced to protect the privacy of third party information. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. Pro Tem Judge: Elizabeth Riles, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/mjm
